summaryText: Guest Speaker Manny Arango opened with a powerful moment of worship, acknowledging God as the one who opens and closes doors in our lives. He then taught from the story of Jacob, Leah, and Rachel in Genesis 29, using it as a metaphor for the choices we make between what is attractive but unfruitful (Rachel) and what may seem unattractive but is deeply fruitful (Leah). Manny shared vulnerably from his own life, including his difficult upbringing with a father who was addicted and incarcerated, to illustrate how God can transform painful seeds into powerful fruit. He emphasized that every believer already possesses the seeds—our words, tears, time, money, and life itself—needed to cultivate the life God intends. The sermon was a call to move from a victim mentality to one of victory, to steward our God-given resources wisely, and to invest in the often difficult but eternally rewarding work of spiritual growth. Manny concluded by inviting the congregation to a practical step of discipleship through the Arma platform, reinforcing the message that we have everything we need to be fruitful for God's kingdom.
mainPoints: God has given you the seeds—your words, tears, time, money, and life—to cultivate the fruitful life He intends for you.
We must choose to invest in the 'Leah' (the unattractive but fruitful work of prayer, fasting, and obedience) over the 'Rachel' (the attractive but empty shortcuts of the world).
Moving from a victim mentality to a victorious identity in Christ requires stewarding our seeds and dying to self so that God can produce life in and through us.

versesMentioned.text: Then Jacob said to Laban, 'Give me my wife. My time is completed, and I want to make love to her.'... When morning came, there was Leah! So Jacob said to Laban, 'What is this you have done to me? I served you for Rachel, didn’t I? Why have you deceived me?'
This is to my Father’s glory, that you bear much fruit, showing yourselves to be my disciples.
Those who sow with tears will reap with songs of joy.

The Lord said to Samuel, 'How long will you mourn for Saul, since I have rejected him as king over Israel? Fill your horn with oil and be on your way; I am sending you to Jesse of Bethlehem. I have chosen one of his sons to be king.'
Very truly I tell you, unless a kernel of wheat falls to the ground and dies, it remains only a single seed. But if it dies, it produces many seeds.

Monday.scripture: Proverbs 18:21 (NIV) - 'The tongue has the power of life and death, and those who love it will eat its fruit.'
Monday.action: Intentionally speak life over yourself and your family today. Refuse to gossip or speak negatively.
Monday.prayer: Lord, guard my mouth today. Let my words be seeds of life, hope, and blessing that reflect Your truth over my circumstances.

Tuesday.scripture: Psalm 56:8 (NIV) - 'Record my misery; list my tears on your scroll—are they not in your record?'
Tuesday.action: Take a moment to pray for something that breaks God's heart—a lost friend, a struggling family, or your city.
Tuesday.prayer: God, give me Your heart. Let my emotions be aligned with Yours. I sow my tears as a prayer for Your kingdom to come.

Wednesday.scripture: Ephesians 5:15-16 (NIV) - 'Be very careful, then, how you live—not as unwise but as wise, making the most of every opportunity, because the days are evil.'
Wednesday.action: Invest 12 minutes today reading your Bible or listening to a biblical teaching instead of scrolling social media.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, help me steward my time wisely. Show me what to set aside so I can sow into knowing You and Your Word more deeply.

Thursday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 9:6 (NIV) - 'Remember this: Whoever sows sparingly will also reap sparingly, and whoever sows generously will also reap generously.'
Thursday.action: Review your spending this week. Make one intentional financial decision that sows into God's kingdom (e.g., giving, blessing someone).
Thursday.prayer: Jesus, all I have is Yours. Give me a generous and faithful heart to sow my finances into what brings eternal fruit and glorifies You.

Friday.scripture: Romans 12:1 (NIV) - 'Therefore, I urge you, brothers and sisters, in view of God’s mercy, to offer your bodies as a living sacrifice, holy and pleasing to God—this is your true and proper worship.'
Friday.action: Identify one area of your life (a habit, pride, comfort) you need to 'die to' today in order to live more fully for Christ.
Friday.prayer: Holy Spirit, I surrender my life to You again. I choose to die to my selfish desires so that Your life can grow in me and through me for others.

Saturday.scripture: Galatians 6:9 (NIV) - 'Let us not become weary in doing good, for at the proper time we will reap a harvest if we do not give up.'
Saturday.action: Reflect on your week. Write down one way you saw fruit from sowing a 'seed' (word, time, etc.) and one area you want to improve.
Saturday.prayer: God, thank You for the seeds You've entrusted to me. Help me to be a faithful and fruitful steward, trusting that You will bring the harvest in Your perfect time.
